quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, which you can see from Port Angeles, is named after a Greek sailor who sailed for Spain in 1592. His real name was Ioannis Phokas, but the Spanish called him Juan de Fuca! He claimed to have discovered this waterway between what is now Washington and British Columbia.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Hummingbirds
short_read.teaser: These tiny jewels of the bird world can fly backwards and their hearts beat over 1,200 times per minute. Learn about the remarkable abilities that make hummingbirds such special visitors to Pacific Northwest gardens.

medium_read.title: The Return of the Elwha River
medium_read.teaser: After the largest dam removal project in U.S. history, the Elwha River near Port Angeles is healing beautifully. Salmon have returned, forests are regenerating, and the river flows free again for the first time in over a century.

long_read.title: Budapest: The Pearl of the Danube
long_read.teaser: A beautiful journey through Hungary's capital city, from its thermal baths and grand architecture to the Danube River that divides Buda and Pest. This photo essay captures the timeless beauty of the city's bridges, cafes, and historic neighborhoods.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot Olympic black-tailed deer at Hurricane Ridge, especially in the early morning or evening. These gentle creatures are unique to the Pacific Northwest and often graze in the subalpine meadows with their adorable spotted fawns this time of year. The drive up to Hurricane Ridge offers breathtaking views of the Olympic Mountains and the Strait of Juan de Fuca.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's wings can beat approximately ___ times per second.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That's incredibly fast — too quick for our eyes to see!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 80 beats per second — that's what creates their distinctive humming sound!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: It's actually about 80 times per second! That's why they make that wonderful humming sound and why their wings appear as just a blur.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: This incredibly fast wing movement allows hummingbirds to hover in place and even fly backwards — the only birds that can do this! The rapid beating also creates the humming sound that gives them their name.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'hello' or 'goodbye'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Szia! You're absolutely right! It's one of the most useful words in Hungarian — friendly and casual.
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is 'Szia' (pronounced see-ah)! It's a wonderfully versatile word you can use for both hello and goodbye with friends.
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Szia is the informal way to greet someone in Hungarian, while 'Jó napot' is more formal. Hungarians often use 'Szia' with family and friends, making it warm and personal.

puzzle_3.question: What animal was reintroduced to Olympic National Park in 2008 after being hunted to extinction there in the early 1900s?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Fishers are back in the Olympics! These beautiful members of the weasel family had been gone for nearly a century, and now they're thriving again in the mountain forests.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It was actually the fisher — a cat-sized member of the weasel family! Between 2008 and 2010, 90 fishers were released into Olympic National Park, and they've been successfully repopulating the forests.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Fishers are skilled tree climbers and one of the few predators that can hunt porcupines. The reintroduction program has been a wonderful success, with fishers now breeding and raising their young throughout the Olympic Peninsula.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
